action.reasoning: Observation: best hidden-test LPIPS is still 0.3385 from cam_unet_v3, and the latest failure is a state_dict mismatch when resuming. Analysis: the old v3 checkpoint was trained with the earlier architecture (inc/down/up naming), while current model.py defines a different architecture (in_proj/down1/down2...). So resume cannot work by compatibility hacks alone; we need to restore the exact old architecture for loading old checkpoints, while keeping predict.py able to load both old and new schemas. The fastest high-value move is to add a legacy model class matching cam_unet_v3, make train/predict instantiate the correct class from checkpoint metadata, then run a short low-LR fine-tune from cam_unet_v3 and smoke test locally. Decision: patch model.py/train.py/predict.py for dual-architecture support, fine-tune 8 epochs from cam_unet_v3 into cam_unet_v4, run local prediction smoke test, and if successful submit next iteration.
action.command: python train.py --resume checkpoints/cam_unet_v3.pt --output checkpoints/cam_unet_v4.pt --epochs 8 --lr 5e-5 --batch_size 16 --num_workers 4 && python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/cam_unet_v4.pt --output local_cam_val_predictions_v4.json
